items.title: Young investors accept 'party had to end' but rethink plans after CGT changes

children.content: Some young Australians locked out of the property market due to unaffordable housing have pursued ETFs and "rentvesting" as a way to build financial security. Here's what they make of the budget's CGT reforms.

gallery.altText: Vanessa seated at table in caravan

thumbnail.altText: Vanessa seated at table in caravan

items.title: Should the tax system reward those who can afford to take risks?

children.content: As the federal budget prompts howls of protest from those who earn the bulk of their income from owning assets rather than working, we shouldn't forget the relative privilege that allowed many of those people to take those risks.

image.altText: A graphic showing a number of currency notes

items.title: Man charged with murder after woman and two children found dead in Sydney home

children.content: A man, 47, has been charged after three people were found dead in a home in Sydney's south-west in what police allege is a domestic violence-related incident.

image.altText: nsw police officers at a crime scne in campbelltown after three people were found dead inside a house

items.title: Elon Musk loses lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman

children.content: The Tesla boss accused OpenAI of wrongfully trying to enrich investors and insiders at the nonprofit's expense, and failing to prioritise AI's safety.

image.altText: A composite image showing Elon Musk and Sam Altman's faces.

items.title: Israeli forces intercept Gaza flotilla with Australians on board, organisers say

children.content: The organisers of a flotilla of ships trying to break Israel's maritime blockade of Gaza say Israel's navy is intercepting the group off the coast of Cyprus.

image.altText: three people in life jackets on a boat

items.title: 'Two years to live': Why we used our super for weight loss surgery

children.content: Weight loss surgery has become an increasingly common option for people grappling with obesity — and many are funding it using retirement savings.

items.title: Overseas company makes billions in profit but is receiving millions in government bailouts

children.content: China is "playing a hard game" with its stranglehold on the global minerals and metals market and Australian smelters need taxpayer support to "play hard back".

image.altText: Two large white buildings at a metal smelter by a port.

items.title: Two men arrested over stunt at Punch the monkey's enclosure

children.content: One of the men was detained by a zoo keeper and arrested after allegedly climbing a fence and breaking into the enclosure.

image.altText: A baby Japanese macaque next to an Ikea stuffed orangutan toy.

items.title: 'I wish I were dead': The heartbreaking toll of Australia's favourite pastime

children.content: Australians are the world's biggest gamblers, losing billions every year. But the true cost goes far beyond the financial.

items.title: Divers to begin 'demanding' recovery of bodies in underwater cave in Maldives

children.content: The bodies of four missing Italian scuba divers who died last week have been found in an underwater cave in the Maldives.

image.altText: Side-by-side images of a mother (left) and her daughter.

items.title: Journalist in London stabbed at behest of Iran, court told

children.content: Pouria Zaratifoukolaei, who works for a Persian-language outlet critical of the Iranian government, was attacked near his home in London in 2024.

image.altText: A court sketch of two men with dark hair wearing smart attire behind persplex glass.

items.title: See where climate change is hitting Victoria's infrastructure hardest

children.content: At least $57 billion worth of infrastructure in Victoria is vulnerable to more frequent and severe bushfires, floods and extreme heat. This figure is expected to grow.

items.title: 'Thriller' history book about the bark petitions wins $50k literary prize

children.content: The 1963 Näku Dhäruk or Yirrkala bark petitions led to Australia's first land rights law. A new book about them just won two prizes at the NSW Literary Awards.

image.altText: Clare Wright, 57, long blonde hair and glasses, arms folded, smiles brightly at the camera as she stands in a library.

items.title: Spanish PM's Socialists party on notice after historic defeat in stronghold

children.content: Voters in the region of Andalusia have sent the ruling Spain's Socialists yet another warning, adding to concerns Pedro Sanchez's government could suffer an electoral wipeout in a nationwide vote.

image.altText: Pedro Sanchez on a campaign stage clapping and smiling.

items.title: Two German film students track down their stolen movie in new doco

children.content: What do you do when your stolen film wins a slew of awards for the thief? In the case of these filmmakers, you travel across the globe to hunt him down.

items.title: Rising costs can put pressure on a relationship. Here's how to address it

children.content: While financial tension between partners isn't always about money, many people clash over values, spending habits, and how they respond to financial pressure.

children.content: The MV Hondius and its crew has docked at the port of Rotterdam in the Netherlands.

items.title: US conducts additional strikes against IS militants in Nigeria

children.content: The US Africa Command announced the strikes days after Trump claimed to have killed the group's second-in-command.

items.title: Blanchett says #MeToo reckoning in Hollywood 'got killed very quickly'

children.content: The Oscar-winning actor says Hollywood's reckoning over sexual misconduct and gender equality has stalled despite the global impact of #MeToo.

image.altText: A photo of Cate Blanchett posing at a film festival. She has short blonde hair and is wearing a black dress with a frilly neck.

items.title: How netball's 'super' veteran left retirement to break 12-year record

children.content: Romelda Aiken-George is supposed to be retired. Instead, the 37-year-old goal shooter is breaking all kinds of records and reinventing herself in her 19th season. After her 255th national league game on the weekend, she reveals the secret to her longevity.

image.altText: A player walks through a guard of honour and waves to the crowd with tears in her eyes

items.title: A Canberra woman's husband said not to book return flights. It was a case of exit trafficking

children.content: Advocates for migrant women say they're seeing more cases of exit trafficking, a little-known crime involving coercion, threats or deception to organise a person's departure from Australia.

items.title: After another fatal shark attack, experts say risk can never be eliminated

children.content: The approach to tackling shark attacks has changed dramatically since the 2013 catch-and-kill policy, with a focus on shark tagging and tracking. But it's never enough to stop fatal attacks.

image.altText: A great white shark under water in the deep blue sea.

items.title: How an Australian designer's fashion fame spurred a mental health unravelling

children.content: Rochelle Gregory has clothed everyone from Paris Hilton to Dua Lipa. Now the designer is using film to candidly explore her mental health battle while showcasing her latest collection.

image.altText: A woman in a black leather jacket struts down a runway while women clap.

items.title: Treasurer says 'death tax' claims are a scare campaign, 'pure and simple'

children.content: The Coalition seizes on the federal government's broken tax promises, claiming a "death tax" has been sneaked into changes to trusts passed on through inheritance. The treasurer says that is a scare campaign.

image.altText: Jim Chalmers in a grey suit

items.title: Horror MotoGP crash leaves rider with broken neck

children.content: Alex Márquez is lucky to be alive after he spectacularly wrecked his MotoGP bike during the Catalan Grand Prix in Barcelona, while Johann Zarco was also taken to hospital after an equally terrifying crash later in the race.

image.altText: Alex Marquez crashes

items.title: Trial begins for concert pianist suing Melbourne Symphony Orchestra

children.content: Pianist Jayson Gillham is suing the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra in the Federal Court over political discrimination after comments he made on stage during a 2024 performance.

items.title: Stronger mouse poison allowed for 'worst plague on record'

children.content: Farm groups have welcomed the chemical regulator's long-awaited decision to approve double-strength mouse baits amid plague numbers in Western Australia and South Australia.

items.title: Qld developer donation overhaul yields $170,000 in cash

children.content: More than $170,000 in donations from developers has been given to Queensland state politicians since laws banning developer donations were overturned in March.

items.title: Parent company orders cattle station to 'cease any plans' for bird cull
